siteInfo.description: Conquer mountains, explore nature, build memories with our community

featuredHikes.title: Sanchez peak
featuredHikes.description: Sanchez Peak is the highest point in General Santos City, standing approximately 800 feet above sea level in Barangay Olympog, offering 360-degree views of Sarangani Bay, Mt. Matutum, and Mt. Parker. It is a popular, beginner-friendly hiking and camping destination featuring breezy, panoramic landscapes and scenic, rolling terrain

featuredHikes.title: Buko-Buko Peak
featuredHikes.description: Buko-Buko Peak is located in Barangay Olympog, General Santos City, South Cotabato, Philippines. Situated in the scenic highland area known as Balakayo, this beginner-friendly, ridge-like hiking spot is often called the 'New Zealand' of the South and offers panoramic views of Sarangani Bay.

expeditions.title: Sanchez Peak
expeditions.description: Beginner-friendly highland hike with wide views of Sarangani Bay, Mt. Matutum, and nearby rolling hills. Good for a short morning or afternoon climb.
budgetItems: Snacks if you want to bring some
Optional entrance or local fees if collected
expeditions.note: Food and gear are personal expenses. Bring your own water and snacks.

expeditions.title: Buko-Buko Peak
expeditions.location: Balakayo, Barangay Olympog, General Santos City
expeditions.description: A relaxed ridge-style hike known for open grassland views and beginner-friendly trails. Best visited when the weather is clear.
budgetItems: Optional entrance or local fees if collected
expeditions.note: This is a cost estimate only, not a package price.

guides.title: Sanchez Peak
guides.description: A beginner-friendly highland trail with open views of Sarangani Bay, Mt. Matutum, and nearby rolling hills. Good for a short morning or afternoon hike.
itinerary: Arrive at the starting point and check the weather
Begin the hike at a steady pace
Take short rest and photo breaks at open viewpoints
Reach the peak area and enjoy the scenery
Descend before it gets too hot or too dark

trailInfo.Weather: Best during clear mornings or late afternoons

safety: Bring enough water because the trail can be exposed
Respect local rules and private areas
Do not leave trash on the trail

guides.title: Buko-Buko Peak
guides.location: Balakayo, Barangay Olympog, General Santos City
guides.description: A relaxed ridge-style hike with open grassland views. It is beginner-friendly and nice for hikers who want a simple scenic trail.
itinerary: Arrive early and prepare water or snacks
Start the walk through the open trail
Take breaks along the ridge viewpoints
Spend time at the peak area for photos
Head back while visibility is still good
essentials: Hat or umbrella for sun protection

trailInfo.Weather: Best when the sky is clear

safety: Be careful on open slopes when it is windy
Bring sun protection because shade can be limited

preparationTips.title: Physical Prep
preparationTips.description: Do light walking or cardio before the hike, especially if the route has steady uphill sections.

preparationTips.title: Gear Check
preparationTips.description: Use comfortable shoes, test your bag, and make sure your water bottle, phone, and essentials are ready.

preparationTips.title: Weather Check
preparationTips.description: Check the forecast before leaving. Clear mornings are usually better for views and safer trail conditions.

preparationTips.title: Food and Water
preparationTips.description: Bring your own water and snacks. Food is not provided, so pack based on your own needs.

testimonials.name: Carlo
testimonials.text: Unforgettable experience. Ang mga bukid kay grabe ka nindot, with strong peaks ug wide views nga dili jud ka magmahay. Nature naa sa tanan dapit, very fresh ug relaxing kaayo ang hangin. Every view kay amazing ug makapahawa sa stress. Simple ra pero powerful kaayo ang feeling, murag connected jud ka sa lugar while nagtan-aw sa palibot.Unforgettable experience. Ang mga bukid kay grabe ka nindot, with strong peaks ug wide views nga dili jud ka magmahay. Nature naa sa tanan dapit, very fresh ug relaxing kaayo ang hangin. Every view kay amazing ug makapahawa sa stress. Simple ra pero powerful kaayo ang feeling, murag connected jud ka sa lugar while nagtan-aw sa palibot.

aboutPage.story: Founded in 2015, Tuktokra began as a small group of mountain enthusiasts sharing their love for adventure. What started as weekend hikes has grown into a vibrant community of over 1,500 passionate hikers and mountaineers. Our mission is to inspire and connect people with nature while promoting sustainable tourism and environmental conservation.
aboutPage.mission: To inspire and empower adventurers to explore the world's most beautiful mountains while building a community dedicated to environmental stewardship and sustainable tourism.
aboutPage.vision: A world where every person experiences the transformative power of mountain adventures and becomes a guardian of natural landscapes.

faq.question: What experience level do I need?
faq.answer: Most listed hikes are beginner-friendly, but always check the difficulty, weather, and your own fitness before going.

faq.question: Is the estimated spend a package price?
faq.answer: No. It is only an estimate of what you might spend for transportation, water, snacks, and possible local fees.

faq.question: What should I bring?
faq.answer: Bring your own water, snacks, comfortable footwear, weather-appropriate clothing, and sun protection.

faq.question: How fit do I need to be?
faq.answer: For easy hikes, basic fitness is usually enough. For longer or steeper routes, prepare with walking or light cardio beforehand.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own food?
faq.answer: Yes. Bring your own snacks or meals if you think you will need them. Food is not provided.

faq.question: Can the cost change?
faq.answer: Yes. Fare, entrance fees, parking, and other local costs can change, so treat the amount as a rough guide.

footer.description: Join few of adventurers exploring the world's most beautiful mountains.
